message:
fix(body): enforce max_size limit before completing on expected_size

AsyncContentLengthStream checked expected_size before max_size,
which meant a request with Content-Length exceeding max_size could
complete successfully if the expected_size check triggered first
(bytes_read >= expected_size). This happened because the size-limit
check was ordered after the completion check in poll_next.

Reorder the checks so max_size is validated first. Add a secondary
budget-exhaustion guard that detects when the remaining body bytes
would exceed the remaining budget (remaining_for_body > 0 but
remaining_budget == 0), emitting TooLarge immediately. Also clamp
chunk reads to remaining_budget for both initial-buffer and reader
paths, preventing any single poll from reading past the limit.

Add async_content_length_stream_enforces_max_size test that sends
12 bytes to a stream with max_size=10 and verifies the first 10
bytes are yielded before a TooLarge error on the next poll.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
